assumptions: No Splunk.com account, Splunk Cloud Platform instance, Splunk Enterprise installation, tutorial data, or indexed event is assumed.
A supported browser and a computer capable of downloading the tutorial ZIP files are available.
No signup, trial creation, terms acceptance, download, installation, upload, or search was performed during research.

developer_goal: Create a Splunk account, choose a trial platform, upload the official tutorial data, and confirm that Splunk indexed it as events.

first_success.outcome: Splunk Cloud indexes an uploaded sample log and the Search app displays it as time-stamped events.
first_success.observable_signal: After the upload Success screen, Start searching opens the Search app with parsed events from the file.

route_selection.rule: Select the lexicographically shortest complete official route to the same first-success outcome after fixing product intent; if peer routes remain consequential, mark needs-human-judgment.

candidates.name: Splunk Search Tutorial
candidates.reason: Recorded as a reconstructable candidate, but public evidence does not close signup-field opacity and/or peer-route lexicographic selection for a verified count.

candidates.name: Splunk Cloud Platform trial
candidates.reason: The Search Tutorial offers this hosted route alongside the self-managed Splunk Enterprise trial and does not establish it as the universal default.

candidates.name: Splunk Enterprise trial
candidates.reason: This route adds an operating-system-specific installer and local installation before Splunk Web, while the tutorial treats it as a peer of Splunk Cloud Platform.

candidates.name: Splunk Observability Cloud
candidates.reason: The official documentation navigation presents Observability Cloud as a separate product surface with its own ingestion and verification journeys.

required_path.action: Complete Splunk account creation or trial signup at the official entry URL.

required_fields.label: Signup identity and required account fields
required_fields.notes: Public evidence does not establish the complete current required field set behind the client-rendered or authenticated signup surface.

required_path.observable_result: A new Splunk account or trial session exists.

required_path.action: Complete any required email or identity verification for the new account.
required_path.observable_result: The account becomes usable for developer onboarding.

required_path.action: Complete the documented post-login setup for the selected Splunk candidate through first success.

required_fields.label: Candidate-specific configuration
required_fields.notes: Exact fields depend on unresolved product, auth, or framework selection.

required_path.observable_result: After the upload Success screen, Start searching opens the Search app with parsed events from the file.

prerequisites.description: {'order': 1, 'type': 'account', 'requirement': 'A Splunk.com account is required to access the free trial software.', 'required': True, 'source_ids': ['S1', 'S2']}

{'order': 2, 'type': 'legal', 'requirement': 'The public account form requires agreement to the Splunk Website Terms and Conditions of Use, Splunk Privacy Policy, and Splunk General Terms.', 'required': True, 'source_ids': ['S2']}
{'order': 3, 'type': 'plan', 'requirement': 'The developer must choose Splunk Cloud Platform or Splunk Enterprise for the tutorial.', 'required': True, 'source_ids': ['S1']}

external_gates.description: The observable state after public account-form submission is not documented.
The Search Tutorial requires a choice between hosted Splunk Cloud Platform and self-managed Splunk Enterprise.
The Cloud candidate requires Splunk Cloud Platform Terms of Service acceptance before the instance opens.

unavoidable_waits.description: Email or identity-provider verification delivery when the selected account route requires it.

platform_outcomes.description: Success! The results confirm that the data in the tutorialdata.zip file was indexed and that events were created.

excluded.reason: Listed as excluded after success in the source record.
Listed as excluded after success in the source record.
Listed as excluded after success in the source record.

uncertainties.question: What exact current required signup, identity, legal, and entitlement fields does Splunk show for a new developer, and which peer route is the lexicographic shortest path?
uncertainties.impact: Without those fields and a closed route choice, action and field counts cannot be verified for peer comparison.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated read-only walkthrough of the live signup and the selected post-login first-success path, with screenshots of required fields and confirmation that peer routes are longer or gated.
